key_assumptions.input: Starting free cash flow per share
key_assumptions.source: LTM distributable earnings (DE) $2.54/sh per Q1-26 interim
FRE +8–10% on fee-bearing capital growth; insurance scale

open_questions: Confirm live price; DE yield vs 5-year range.
Hold override?** 14.28% synthesis vs prior **hold** for allocator sleeve.
Model BN+BNT merger impact on DE per share.
thesis: Brookfield Corporation is a global alternative-asset manager and owner of real-asset platforms. **[Fact]** Institutional clients pay management and performance fees on capital deployed into infrastructure, real estate, credit, and private equity strategies. **[Fact]** Shareholder economics are measured as **distributable earnings (DE)** before and after realizations, not GAAP net income. **[Fact]** Q1 2026 fee-bearing capital was **$614B**; insurance and wealth platform assets **$180B**. **[Fact]** Q1 2026 DE was **$1.55B** (**$0.66/sh**); LTM DE per share **$2.54**. **[Inference]** Realizations are lumpy; base valuation uses DE growth excluding heroic realization years unless in the bull case.
why_market_wrong: There is **no clear dated mispricing signal** (HK predictive attribute: **none**). **[Inference]** Listed holdco complexity and GAAP mark-to-market noise on **~$519.6B** consolidated assets may obscure steady **fee-related earnings (FRE)** growth (+11% YoY Q1). **[Inference]** **BN+BNT simplification** could narrow a persistent holdco discount over time, but timing is uncertain. Return is **DE compounding at price**, not a single liquidation date.
